Pediatric Dentist in Karve Nagar
DR. SWAPNIL K. RACHHA
Dr. Swapnil Rachha is an enthusiastic Pediatric Dentist known for his diligent work practicing in Pune, India. Having completed graduation in dental surgery (BDS) from prestigious Sinhgad Dental College & Hospital, Pune and post-graduation in Pediatric and Preventive Dentistry (MDS) from MA Rangoonwala College of Dental Science & Research Centre one of the oldest institutes for dental sciences in Pune, Maharashtra. Dr Swapnil Rachha went on to pursue Post Graduate Diploma in Emergency Medical Services (PGD EMS) from Inamdar Hospital which is affiliated to the American Heart Association.
Dr. Swapnil Rachha is the Founder & Chief Dental Surgeon at Soulful Dental Care and is in the dental field since 2007. He is a Certified Pediatric & Preventive Dentist (Childrens Dental Specialist) and is committed to providing the best dental care for every patient and believes in giving honest dental advice and sincere dental treatment to all his patients. He is considered as one of the best dentists in Kothrud and Pune Metropolitan Region by many of his patient recommendations and is especially loved by his child patients.
He is a Proud Member of Indian Society for Pediatric & Preventive Dentists , American Heart Association, Indian Dental Association & Pune Pediatric Dentists Association.
He is one of the founder member & currently working as honorary treasurer at the Pune Pediatric Dentists Association. Dr Rachha is also commited to welfare of underprivileged and specially abled kids and works towards their betterment through organizations like Manavya, Being social and Carbonari.
If you are looking for a trusted Pediatric Dentist in Karve Nagar, Pune, Dr. Swapnil Rachha is the perfect choice for you.

mainEntity.name: When should my child first visit a pediatric dentist?

acceptedAnswer.text: The first dental visit should happen by your child's first birthday or within 6 months of the first tooth erupting — whichever comes first.

mainEntity.name: Is sedation dentistry safe for children?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We use nitrous oxide (laughing gas) which is the safest sedation for children. It takes effect in minutes, wears off in 5 minutes, and has no lasting side effects. Dr. Rachha is sedation-certified.

mainEntity.name: How much does pediatric dental treatment cost in Pune?

acceptedAnswer.text: A basic checkup and cleaning starts at Rs.500-Rs.1,000. Fillings from Rs.800, sealants from Rs.600, crowns from Rs.2,500, and sedation from Rs.1,500 per session.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between a pedodontist and a general dentist?

acceptedAnswer.text: A pedodontist holds an MDS degree with 3 extra years of specialization in children's dentistry including behavior management, sedation, and pediatric orthodontics.

mainEntity.name: Should decayed milk teeth be treated or extracted?

acceptedAnswer.text: Milk teeth must be preserved whenever possible. Early extraction causes neighboring teeth to shift, leading to crowded permanent teeth. We treat infected baby teeth with pulpectomy and crown.

[![Why didn’t anyone tell us this earlier?
It’s something we hear from parents more often than you’d think. 💙
A tongue tie is just a small band of tissue under the tongue, but its impact can be surprisingly big.
👶 In babies, it may lead to painful feeding, poor latch, and exhausted parents wondering what they’re doing wrong.
🧒 In older children, it can affect speech, chewing, swallowing, and even the way they naturally clean their teeth.
The difficult part? The signs are often subtle, which means many families spend months, sometimes years, searching for answers.
The reassuring part is that when identified early, tongue tie is usually simple to assess and, if needed, straightforward to treat.
So if your instincts are telling you that feeding isn’t quite right, speech doesn’t seem to be progressing as expected, or something feels off, trust that feeling.

[![Thumb sucking is completely normal in babies and toddlers it’s one of the ways they soothe themselves. 💙
But if the habit continues beyond the age of 4, it can begin to affect the way your child’s mouth develops.
It may lead to:
🦷 Front teeth moving forward
🦷 Narrowing of the upper jaw
🦷 Bite changes
🦷 Speech difficulties in some children
The good news? This isn’t about blame, punishment, or taking comfort away from your child.
The key is understanding why the habit continues. Is it stress? Sleep? Boredom? Once the underlying trigger is addressed, breaking the habit often becomes much easier.
Early guidance can make all the difference and may help avoid more complex treatment later.
If your child is still thumb sucking after age four, we’re here to help you navigate it gently and without judgement. ✨
